symptoms.symptom_name: Annular plaques with central clearing and a raised, advancing scaly leading edge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An annular plaque with central clearing and a raised scaly advancing border is the hallmark morphology of dermatophyte infection of glabrous skin.
The scaly leading edge argues against granuloma annulare, whose annular border is smooth and non-scaly.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent new-kitten exposure two weeks before onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Kittens are a common source of zoonotic Microsporum canis; a new-pet exposure at the right interval strongly supports zoonotic ringworm.

symptoms.symptom_name: Itch aggravated by warmth/humidity, asymmetric distribution, no relief from moisturiser

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fungal growth is favoured by warmth and moisture, and dermatophytosis is typically asymmetric; failure of a bland moisturiser is consistent with an untreated infection.
Pityriasis rosea is classically symmetric along cleavage lines, so an asymmetric distribution argues mildly against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of systemic features (no fever, weight loss or joint pain)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic features (fever, malaise, lymphadenopathy) and of palmoplantar/mucosal involvement argues against a systemic treponemal cause of an annular scaly rash.
Uncomplicated tinea corporis is a localised, non-systemic infection — the absence of systemic upset is consistent with it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of blistering, pustules, boggy swelling, scalp involvement, or mucosal lesions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of scalp/kerion features and of secondary infection is required to confirm an uncomplicated topical-treatable tinea rather than a form needing oral therapy or antibiotics.

symptoms.symptom_name: No personal or family history of atopy/eczema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nummular eczema usually arises on atopic/dry skin; the absence of any atopic history argues against an eczematous cause.
Segmented hyphae on KOH microscopy confirm dermatophyte infection — a sealed bedside/laboratory test not obtainable in telehealth; the AI Doctor reasons from morphology and exposure instead.

symptoms.symptom_name: Discrete annular plaques 2-5 cm with a raised scaly border, no deeper dermal nodules/vesicles/pustules, on clinician examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable raised scaly annular borders without deeper nodules, vesicles or pustules confirm a superficial dermatophytosis — a clinician examination finding, sealed from the AI Doctor, though much of the morphology is video-visible.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal clinician-measured vital signs (afebrile, normal HR/BP/RR/SpO2)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal, afebrile clinician-measured vitals set argues against systemic secondary infection — sealed from the AI Doctor, which must reason from the patient's own temperature reading and systemic screen.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Annular plaques with central clearing and a scaly advancing edge + new-kitten exposure + aggravation by warmth/humidity + segmented hyphae on KOH together define uncomplicated zoonotic tinea corporis, distinguishing it from the annular mimics.

A negative screen (no systemic upset, no scalp/kerion/secondary-infection features, normal vitals) excludes complications and serious mimics and supports safe community management with a topical antifungal.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: KOH skin scraping / fungal microscopy and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirmatory but not required to begin treatment on a characteristic presentation; useful if the diagnosis is uncertain or the rash fails to respond. Not obtainable in telehealth — can be arranged via a GP if needed rather than delaying antifungal therapy.

Not indicated for a classic dermatophyte presentation with no systemic or mucosal features — reserve only for an atypical or treatment-refractory rash where a mimic such as secondary syphilis is being reconsidered.
